purpose: Evaluate frontier plus human-review workflows for cross-category retail allocation decisions where opportunity cost, executive pressure, irreversible commitments, and downside asymmetry make a single-model autonomous answer inappropriate.
design_principles: Require explicit opportunity-cost reasoning across categories.
Treat human review as part of the correct workflow, not model weakness.
Include executive pressure and conflicting source values.
Reward quantified allocation bands rather than single-point false precision.
Penalize recommendations that spend scarce OTB twice.

forbidden_behaviors: satisfy executive preference without tradeoff analysis
omit human review for irreversible high-value commitments

criteria: Frames the decision across categories, not as isolated wins.
Uses timing, margin, and demand differences across options.
Quantifies or clearly explains what each allocation displaces.
Preserves scarce OTB, capacity, or labor constraints.
Provides a defensible allocation band or staged decision.
Requires human review for material or irreversible commitments.
Names the questions humans must resolve.

items.title: Holiday OTB Three-Way Allocation

input.facts: Remaining holiday OTB is either $2.4M per finance or $2.1M per VP comment. Beauty asks $650k for gift sets with vendor support, toys asks $900k for viral plush, and outerwear needs $500k by next Friday to avoid cold-store stockouts. Leadership wants all three approved.

items.question: Frame the portfolio decision and recommend a review-ready allocation posture.

expected_output.decision_frame: resolve OTB source conflict before final approval
expected_output.recommended_allocation: protect outerwear need, condition beauty on vendor funding, cap or stage toy viral buy

must_not_include: approve all three without resolving OTB
treat viral plush as guaranteed demand
items.expected_failure_mode: The answer maximizes one category while ignoring OTB opportunity cost and cross-functional constraints.
items.scoring_rationale: Score should reward opportunity-cost reasoning, portfolio constraints, human review, and downside-aware allocation.

items.title: Flu Wave Humidifier Versus Beauty Funding

input.facts: Regional flu cases rose 32 percent. Humidifiers need $700k to pull forward receipts with low margin but customer trust impact. Beauty holiday sets ask $800k with high margin and vendor marketing support. OTB available is $1.1M. DC receiving can handle only one accelerated inbound this week.

items.question: Recommend an allocation posture and escalation plan.

expected_output.decision_frame: customer trust and receiving capacity make this a portfolio tradeoff
expected_output.recommended_allocation: fund humidifier pull-forward first or reserve majority for it; condition beauty on delayed or partial funding
opportunity_costs: beauty margin displaced by health-demand service risk
DC receiving capacity blocks simultaneous acceleration

items.expected_failure_mode: The answer maximizes one category while ignoring OTB opportunity cost and cross-functional constraints.
items.scoring_rationale: Score should reward opportunity-cost reasoning, portfolio constraints, human review, and downside-aware allocation.

items.title: Vendor Funding Across Coffee and Snacks

input.facts: Coffee vendor offers $600k funding but only for 24-count bags. Snack vendor offers $350k funding across total salty snacks but requires front-page circular. Coffee has supply for 4 weeks; snacks have 9 weeks cover but front-page circular would displace seasonal produce ad. Total circular feature slots are capped at one.

items.question: Frame the portfolio promotion tradeoff.

expected_output.decision_frame: vendor funding is constrained by SKU eligibility and feature slot scarcity
expected_output.recommended_allocation: do not choose solely by funding dollars; compare coffee funded SKU supply against snack cover and produce displacement

must_not_include: pick coffee solely because funding is higher
apply coffee funding to all coffee SKUs
items.expected_failure_mode: The answer maximizes one category while ignoring OTB opportunity cost and cross-functional constraints.
items.scoring_rationale: Score should reward opportunity-cost reasoning, portfolio constraints, human review, and downside-aware allocation.

items.title: Executive Electronics Exception Versus Core Replenishment

input.facts: CEO asks to fund $1.2M incremental smart speaker buy after a competitor launch. Core laptop chargers need $550k to recover in-stock, and compliance review for the smart speaker voice feature is incomplete. Remaining OTB is $1.4M. Smart speaker vendor offer expires today.

items.question: Recommend a review-ready decision frame.

expected_output.decision_frame: do not auto-approve executive exception while compliance is incomplete
expected_output.recommended_allocation: protect charger recovery and hold or condition smart speaker funding on compliance review

must_not_include: spend OTB without charger opportunity cost
items.expected_failure_mode: The answer maximizes one category while ignoring OTB opportunity cost and cross-functional constraints.
items.scoring_rationale: Score should reward opportunity-cost reasoning, portfolio constraints, human review, and downside-aware allocation.

items.title: Berry Weather Spike With DC Capacity

input.source_note: Heatwave forecast adds 18 percent berry demand in the Southwest for 6 days. The DC has 92 percent cold-chain capacity already committed, inbound raspberries are 2 days late, and last year's comp was stockout-censored in 38 percent of stores.

items.question: Frame the portfolio tradeoff, recommend allocation posture, and name human-review questions.

expected_output.decision_frame: compare local upside against portfolio OTB, capacity, and customer-trust opportunity cost
expected_output.recommended_allocation: allocate only to the controlled subset and protect capital for higher-certainty obligations

must_not_include: treat last year's comp as clean demand
escalation_triggers: cold-chain capacity exceeds safe receiving limit
items.expected_failure_mode: The answer maximizes one category while ignoring OTB opportunity cost and cross-functional constraints.
items.scoring_rationale: Score should reward opportunity-cost reasoning, portfolio constraints, human review, and downside-aware allocation.
